Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Nathalia Aguirre, Esq. of Patenaude & Felix, A.P.C. (tel: 858-244-7600) Credit Corp Solutions Inc., Assignee of Synchrony Bank.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT JASON JONES' Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant and cross-complainant Jason Jones's motion for leave to amend his cross-complaint is granted. Leave to amend is liberally granted. (See, e.g., Morgan v. Superior Court (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530 ["it is a rare case in which a court will be justified in refusing a party leave to amend his pleadings so that he may properly present his case"] [simplified].) Generally, the only basis to deny a plausible amendment is where there is prejudice.

Here, trial is set for January 2026 and there is sufficient time to prepare for trial. Delay alone does not constitute prejudice. Cross-defendant contends that the proposed amended pleading is insufficient but these concerns may be raised on demurrer. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 760 ["we believe that the better course of action would have been to allow Atkinson to amend the complaint and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings"].)

Jones shall file the proposed amended pleading by August 22, 2025.

Prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. NATHALIA AGUIRRE, Esq. of Patenaude & Felix, A.P.C. (tel: 858-244-7600) for Plaintiff / Cross-Defendant Credit Corp Solutions Inc.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., ASSIGNEE OF SYNCHRONY BANK's DEMURRER to Amended CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

A demurrer lies only as to an entire cause of action. The court must liberally construe the pleading pursuant to CCP 452.

Plaintiff Jason Jones's first cause of action alleges a violation of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, 15 USC 1692 et seq. Jones alleges that he submitted a written dispute to Credit Corp of the debt on November 18, 2023 and that Credit Corp's response was to send him a generic "debt validation" letter. (SACC paras. 5-6.) The court concludes this is sufficient to state a claim. Credit Corp's contrary arguments conflate the requirements of 15 USC 1692g(a) with 1692g(b). Section 1692g(b) provides that where a consumer disputes the validity of a debt within 30 days, the debt collector must mail "verification of the debt or a copy of a judgment." That is required even where the original notice of debt included the amount of the debt, the name of the creditor, and the name of the original creditor. Jones adequately alleges that Credit Corp did not mail "verification of the debt" as required. Because Jones has adequately alleged at least one violation of the FDCPA, the court need not consider Credit Corp's other objections to this cause of action.

Because the first cause of action is adequately pleaded, so is the second cause of action for a violation of the Rosenthal Act, Civil Code 1788 et seq. As Credit Corp acknowledges, the Rosenthal Act mirrors and incorporates the substantive provisions of the FDCPA.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Defendant'S Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Mar-27-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JASON JONES' Motion To Reconsider Defendant's Motion To Dismiss.

Defendant moves this court to reconsider its February 14, 2025 order that took his motion to dismiss off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies. Local Rule 2.7(B)(5) provides that "Failure to lodge courtesy copies of moving Papers as required by statute, rule or court order may, in the discretion of the judicial officer presiding over the hearing, result in denial of the motion, continuance of the hearing, or taking the motion off calendar." In this case, the court took the matter off calendar, which means no decision was reached on the motion.

Defendant filed a supplemental notice of motion on March 7 recognizing the above and asking the court to consider the motion to dismiss as a new filing (but nonetheless not providing courtesy copies of the moving papers on the motion to dismiss). The supplemental notice was not timely filed. (CCP 1005(b) [moving papers must be served and filed at least 16 court days before hearing].) Plaintiff did not respond to the supplemental notice or the merits of the motion to dismiss but instead argued that there was no basis for reconsideration.

The court denies the relief requested by Defendant-an order of dismissal-but notes that Defendant may refile his motion to dismiss as a new motion and may obtain a hearing by complying with Local Rule 2.7(B). The court's procedural rules help it to manage its large caseload; they may seem onerous to individual litigants but they serve the efficient administration of justice across the run of cases.

Defendant also requests that the court bifurcate his cross-complaint to permit him to proceed on the cross-complaint separately. The court denies this request without prejudice to any determination by the trial judge to bifurcate.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Credit Corp. Solutions Inc.'s demurrer to Defendant and Cross-Complainant Jason Jones' Cross-Complaint to which no opposition was filed is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Neither the first nor the second cause of action constitutes a claim upon which relief can be granted, especially if the allegations regarding Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant litigation conduct are removed as bases for liability, as they must be pursuant to the litigation privilege. (Civ. Code, Sec. 47, subd. (b)(2) "A privileged publication or broadcast is one made [] (b) In any[] (2) judicial proceeding."]; Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal.3d 205, 212 The usual formulation is that the privilege applies to any communication (1) made in judicial or quasi-judicial proceedings; (2) by litigants or other participants authorized by law; (3) to achieve the objects of the litigation; and (4) that have some connection or logical relation to the action.]; Gallegos v. Pac. Lumber Co. (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 950, 958, citing Silberg , supra, 50 Cal.3d at 212 [The litigation privilege "renders absolutely privileged communications made as part of a 'judicial or quasi-judicial proceeding."'].)

Intervenor is granted 20 days leave to amend from the time of notice of the order on this demurrer.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
